OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":34,"gender":"woman","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"plumber","city_of_living":"Los Angeles","employment_status":"self-employed","education_level":"upper secondary","parents_education":{"parent_1":"bachelor's degree","parent_2":"vocational diploma"},"current_marital_status":"in a relationship","children":2,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"curious","religious_beliefs":"Christianity","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend several hours daily on social media","hobbies":["yoga","volunteering","cycling","videogames"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":20,"level":"low"},"conscientiousness":{"score":48,"level":"moderate"},"extraversion":{"score":74,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":49,"level":"moderate"},"neuroticism":{"score":11,"level":"low"}}},"opinion":"As a plumber in Los Angeles, I'm used to dealing with practical, hands-on situations, and I bring that same no-nonsense approach to discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines. I believe in facts, science, and looking after my community, which is why I got vaccinated and encourage others to do the same. I see the vaccine as a way to protect my family, my customers, and myself. I've had conversations with customers who are hesitant, and I approach these discussions with empathy and understanding. I share my personal reasons for getting vaccinated, like wanting to keep my kids safe and being able to work without worrying about getting sick. I also make sure to listen to their concerns and provide them with reliable information from sources like the CDC or WHO. I avoid getting into heated arguments because I know that won't change anyone's mind. Instead, I focus on being a good listener and sharing my experiences. I also try to set a good example by following safety guidelines, like wearing a mask when necessary and getting my vaccines on schedule. I believe that by being open, honest, and compassionate, I can help others make informed decisions about their health. I also think it's important to respect that everyone has the right to make their own choices, even if I don't agree with them. At the end of the day, I want to do what's best for my community, and that means promoting health and safety in a way that's respectful and understanding.","reasoning_summary":"Given my background as a practical, hands-on worker, I value facts and science. My Christian beliefs and role as a parent also influence my views on healthcare. I spend a lot of time on social media, which exposes me to various opinions. My high extraversion and moderate agreeableness make me approach discussions openly and empathetically. I avoid conflict and focus on sharing personal experiences and reliable information. I respect others' choices while promoting health and safety.","tone":"practical, empathetic, respectful","citations":["CDC","WHO"],"safety_notes":"none","used_persona_fields":["occupation","city_of_living","education_level","current_marital_status","children","psychological_feature","religious_beliefs","time_onsocialmedia","hobbies","ocean"]}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a plumber in Los Angeles, I'm used to dealing with practical, hands-on situations, and I bring that same no-nonsense approach to discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines. I believe in facts, science, and looking after my community, which is why I got vaccinated and encourage others to do the same. I see the vaccine as a way to protect my family, my customers, and myself. I've had conversations with customers who are hesitant, and I approach these discussions with empathy and understanding. I share my personal reasons for getting vaccinated, like wanting to keep my kids safe and being able to work without worrying about getting sick. I also make sure to listen to their concerns and provide them with reliable information from sources like the CDC or WHO. I avoid getting into heated arguments because I know that won't change anyone's mind. Instead, I focus on being a good listener and sharing my experiences. I also try to set a good example by following safety guidelines, like wearing a mask when necessary and getting my vaccines on schedule. I believe that by being open, honest, and compassionate, I can help others make informed decisions about their health. I also think it's important to respect that everyone has the right to make their own choices, even if I don't agree with them. At the end of the day, I want to do what's best for my community, and that means promoting health and safety in a way that's respectful and understanding.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Given my background as a practical, hands-on worker, I value facts and science. My Christian beliefs and role as a parent also influence my views on healthcare. I spend a lot of time on social media, which exposes me to various opinions. My high extraversion and moderate agreeableness make me approach discussions openly and empathetically. I avoid conflict and focus on sharing personal experiences and reliable information. I respect others' choices while promoting health and safety.
